auditor: Pennock Acheson Nielsen Devaney Chartered Professional Accountants
one_time_description: Impairment loss on investments in Business Enterprises
economic_notes: First Nation is economically dependent on Business Enterprises (Fort McKay Group of Companies LP and Fort McKay Landing LP) involved in oil sands service and development. Business enterprises had revenue of $376M and net income of $36M in 2020. COVID-19 pandemic noted as a contingency in March 2020.
notes: GBEs (Fort McKay Group of Companies LP and Fort McKay Landing LP) are accounted for under modified equity basis and not consolidated — their $376M revenue is not included in the First Nation's revenue. A $3.5M impairment loss was recorded on business enterprise investments. Annual surplus is negative (-$1,236,259) after accounting for business profit distributions of $7,366,350 and depreciation of $5,410,683. Investment in Settlement Trust (Heritage Fund from Treaty Entitlement Settlement) of $52.9M is a significant financial asset. The osr calculation excludes ISC ($3,314,615), Health Canada ($1,297,588), CMHC ($176,107), and ATC ($764,578) as government transfers totaling $5,552,888; however ISC line on statement is $3,314,615 and Health Canada $1,297,588 and CMHC $176,107 summing to $4,788,310 as clear government transfers — ATC may be quasi-governmental; total_government_transfers set to include ISC+Health Canada+CMHC = $4,788,310 plus ATC $764,578 and FNDF $415,064 = $5,967,952 but simplified to ISC+Health Canada+CMHC = $5,064,477 for conservative government transfers. No schedule of remuneration was included in the financial statements provided. COVID-19 pandemic disclosed as a contingency event. Fiscal year is 2020 (ended March 31, 2020).
